feed.title: SAP Community - API Management
feed.subtitle: API Management Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Instance creation for apiportal-apiaccess plan in btp trial fails
entry.content:
Hi , 

I am trying to create instance for my tria account, but it fails with below error. I was able to create the intance in 2024, but now It is not working anymore. Am I missing anything? I have subscribed to Integration suite and able to create api , public and view in the dev portal. 

https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/api-access-plan-for-api-portal


Creation 

Error 

Couldn't create an instance of service 'API Management, API portal' with plan 'apiportal-apiaccess'.
 

 

 


Deletion

Couldn't delete service instance 'api-trial'.
Instance ID: b5266bf9-d15d-4caf-8d46-cbd99734d134

Service instance api-trial: Service broker error: Service broker apimanagement-apiportal-trial failed with: Forbidden

Regards,

Muni

entry.title: How to configure SAP IAS as a proxy IDP for Principal Propagation to SuccessFactors via APIM
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are currently working on integrating an external system to our SAP SuccessFactors , enabling Principal Propagation using SAP API Management (APIM).
In our case , SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS) is acting as a proxy to our actual Identity Provider (IDP).

While implementing the workaround described in SAP KBA 3462930 (OAuthSAMLBearer Token Exchange Bundle), we need to configure the following parameters in SFSFContainer-fortemplate \APIProxy\FileResource\config.js - File:

 

  • sapapim.sf.samlissuer – Trusted Identity Provider (IDP) entity ID

  • sapapim.sf.keystorename – Name of the Keystore in SAP API Management

  • sapapim.sf.certname – Name of the Certificate stored inside that Keystore

We would appreciate guidance on the following points:

  1. When SAP IAS is acting as a proxy IDP, what value should be set for sapapim.sf.samlissuer? Should it be the entity ID of IAS itself, or the underlying corporate IDP?

  2. How to generate the certificate in SAP IAS and upload it into SAP API Management’s keystore for Principal Propagation.

  3. Is there an official guide or best practices for setting up SAP IAS as a proxy for Principal Propagation through SAP API Management towards SuccessFactors?

Any advice, step-by-step guidance, or reference links would be highly appreciated.

Thank you very much for your help!

entry.title: How to send Excel file as an attachment in body in API?
entry.content:
How to send Excel file as an attachment in body in API? 

API API Management SAP S/4HANA Cloud for credit integration (Foundry) ABAP Connectivity SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: Accessing SAP Graph OData V4 through SAP Cloud Integration (CPI)
entry.content:
Introduction

This blog explains how to access an SAP Graph OData V4 URL using SAP Cloud Integration (CPI). We will configure the connection, use OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials, and query data using OData parameters.

What is SAP Graph?

SAP Graph is a unified API that provides a simplified, single interface to access data across various SAP systems (like SAP S/4HANA, SAP SuccessFactors, SAP Sales Cloud, etc.). It helps developers interact with complex SAP systems using a consistent API structure, making integration and data access easier.

Prerequisites

  • Access to SAP BTP with an SAP Graph instance.

  • OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials for SAP Graph.

  • SAP CPI with design privileges.

Step 1: Create OAuth 2.0 Credentials

  1. In SAP BTP Cockpit, navigate to your SAP Graph instance.

  2. Go to Security and create OAuth 2.0 client credentials.

  3. Note the Client ID and Client Secret.

  4. Save the credentials in Security Material of CPI as a new OAuth2 Client Credentials artifact (e.g., Credential Name: GraphOAuthCredentials).

Step 2: Get the SAP Graph URL

  1. In SAP Integration Suite, navigate to the Design section.

  2. Inside Business Data Graph, you can find the OData V4 URL for your SAP Graph instance.

  3. Copy the OData V4 URL (e.g., https://<your-graph-url>/graph/api/graphdemo).

Step 3: Configure OData Connection in CPI

  1. Log in to SAP CPI, go to Design, and create an Integration Flow.

  2. Add a Start Timer and a Request Reply step.

    3. Set connection details:

  • Address: https://<your-graph-url>/graph/api/graphdemo/sap.s4

  • Authentication: OAuth2 Client Credentials

  • Credential Name: GraphOAuthCredentials

 

Step 4: Set the Resource Path

 

  • Resource Path: A_SalesOrder

  • Query: $select=SalesOrder,SoldToParty&$filter=SalesOrder eq '1'&$expand=_SoldToParty

Step 5: Deploy and Test

  1. Deploy the integration flow.

  2. Monitor message processing to verify the response.

Conclusion

You have successfully configured SAP CPI to access SAP Graph OData V4 using OAuth 2.0 authentication.

entry.title: Synchronise Tasks from Jira to SAP CALM
entry.content:
Hallo Team, 

  • I did integrated Jira with CALM, whereby when i create a task in SAP CALM, this will be created in Jira and also updated there, i did follow this instruction and did some Jira Automation to achieve that --> so when i do some changes in CALM, these will be adapted to Jira.
  • Now i want to do the oppposite, so when tasks are created, updated in Jira, these actions should also be adapted to SAP CALM
    • How can i achieve that, is there is an API from Jira to CALM
    • is there is any clear instruction, Guide How to?

Best wishes

Quteiba

entry.title: Unexpected EOF at Target when Calling API Proxy for Standard OData Service via SAP API Management
entry.content:
Hello Community,

I’ve created an API proxy in SAP API Management that wraps an existing standard OData service from our SAP S/4HANA system. The proxy was deployed successfully, and I’ve included the required API Key in the apikey header when testing via Postman.

Steps that I have followed:
1. I selected an API from api.sap.com to fetch/retrieve time-sheet data and tested it in POSTMAN and I was getting the data.
2. I created an API proxy using SAP API Management with included policy of APIkey and published the product.
3.But when I tried calling from postman it is giving this error:

{
  "fault": {
    "faultstring": "Unexpected EOF at target",
    "detail": {
      "errorcode": "messaging.adaptors.http.flow.UnexpectedEOFAtTarget"
    }
  }
}

 

I want a solution to this problem!

Regards,
Shashikant Patel

entry.title: SAP API Management using Entra and OAuth 2.0
entry.content:
Good Morning Community! I was wondering if anyone had experience in attempting to leverage Entra AD (Microsoft Azure) as a source of authentication and bearer token issuance within API Management. I am attempting to use the following as a basis for my trials, but am finding some gaps in the actual setup and build out. Any assistance would be greatly appreciated!

Utilising Third-Party OAuth Tokens in SAP API Mana... - SAP Community

entry.title: API to get PDF output form from sales order
entry.content:
Hello all,

We are looking for a solution to retrieve PDF output form from sales order to be used in SAP Commerce Cloud integration. 

This functionality is available for billing documents (Get PDF of Billing Document | SAP Help Portal)  but is there any API or any other method to send "order confirmation form" to SAP Commerce Cloud from S/4HANA Public Cloud?

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition API Management SAP Commerce Cloud SD (Sales and Distribution)

entry.title: (APIs) TransportationCode -1 not acquired
entry.content:
Hi guys, here again to ask your GURU help.

When I try to PATCH the value -1 for "TransportationCode" (no transport) via SL, the document takes casual values from OSHP table, but not -1.

I tried patching only the document head TransportationCode, but nothing to do, so I tried to patch even the lines combined with the head (as you can see by code below, tried also via PostMan) but results doesn't change: casual values existing in OSHP, also different between head and lines.

{
  "DocEntry": 263,
  "TransportationCode": -1,
  "DocumentLines": [
           {
            "LineNum": 0,
           "ShippingMethod": -1
          }
 ]
}

Instead, if I patch with zero (nonsense, because not in the OSHP...) it is accepted and acquired. 

Does anyone know where I'm wrong?

Thank you.

entry.title: Are OData V2 Services Available in OData V4 in SAP, and Which Ones?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I hope you’re all doing well! I’m currently working on a project involving SAP Gateway and OData services, and I have a question regarding the availability of OData services across different versions.
We are using several OData V2 services in our current SAP system (SAP S/4HANA 2022), and we are planning to transition to OData V4 to leverage its advanced features (e.g., improved metadata, batch processing, and better support for modern APIs). However, I’m unsure whether the OData V2 services we rely on are also available in OData V4, or if we’ll need to redevelop or adjust our integrations.
Here’s what I’d like to know:
1- Are OData V2 services generally available in OData V4 in SAP systems? If not, how can we identify which ones have equivalent V4 versions?
2 - Specifically, we are using the following OData V2 services:

  • API_PRODUCT_SRV – Using for Material Master as a Product
  • API_EQUIPMENT – Using for Equipment Master
  • API_FUNCTIONALLOCATION – Using Functional location
  • API_MAINTENANCEBOM -  Using for Material BOM and Equipment BOM
  • API_BILL_OF_MATERIAL_SRV -  Using for Material BOM
  • API_MAINTENANCEPLAN -  Using for Maintenance Item
  • API_MAINTENANCEPLAN -  Using for Maintenance Plan
  • API_BUSINESS_PARTNER -  Using for Business Partne(Vendor/Customer)
  • API_CLFN_CLASS_SRV – Using for Class
  • API_CLFN_CHARACTERISTIC_SRV – Using for Characteristics
  • API_MAINTNOTIFICATION – Using for Maintenance Notification
  • API_MAINTENANCEORDER -  Using for Maintenance Order

    Can anyone confirm if these services (or their equivalents) are available in OData V4? If yes, what are their technical names in V4, and are there any significant differences in their structure or functionality?

        Is there a systematic way (e.g., a report, transaction, or SAP documentation) to check which OData V2             services have been migrated or reimplemented in ODataV4?

Looking forward to your expertise and guidance. Thank you in advance for your help!

Best regards,

entry.title: API Integration consume API passing attachment as http link for the 3rd party user to download
entry.content:
Hi Gurus,
I am working on api integration for Purchase Requisition between SAP and PROCOL. We have to consume the API shared by the client to transfer the data between SAP and PROCOL. We are doing this integration through PIPO. PIPO Consultant has created interface which we have used it in our abap program.

There is only one point which I could not able to achieve. The PR would be having attachments which I have to send it to PROCOL as http link as shown below in a variable of the nested structure shared by PROCOL.

Is it possible that the image attachment which was associated with the PR would be shared as http link by passing into the structure?

entry.title: Create custom entity with compositions exposed as OData V4 API
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

I have a question which I am not able to solve by myself ^^

My requirement basically is to create a new custom API in SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud that allows me, for a given {warehouse + batch-managed product + quantity}, to find the list of all batch numbers, that can be consumed to address that batch-managed product's quantity based on some rules.

I gave myself as objective to create this structure as output of my new custom API, with a header structure (giving the status of the batch determination logic) and an item list (giving the list of all the batch numbers that should be used for the consumption, with the quantity to be consumed in each batch):

I started by created 2 custom CDS entities: one for the header structure and another one for the item structure.

I added this annotation to both these 2 custom CDS entities "@ObjectModel.query.implementedBy:'ABAP:ZCL_TEST_BATCH_NBR_DET1'" where "ZCL_TEST_BATCH_NBR_DET1'" is a custom class referencing interface "IF_RAP_QUERY_PROVIDER" In order to be able to trigger its method "SELECT" when someone tries to retrieve data from either one of my 2 custom CDS entities. 

In order to have a parent-child relationship between my 2 custom CDS entities, I added a composition statement in the header custom entity CDS view and an association to parent statement in the line-items child custom entity CDS view.

Custom Entity for the Header parent CDS view:

Custom Entity for the Line-Item Child CDS view:

Then I implemented code for the SELECT statement of my custom class ZCL_TEST_BATCH_NBR_DET1.

Once done, I created a new service definition exposing both my 2 custom cds entities:

Then I exposed my service definition into an OData V4 API service binding, followed with the normal communication scenario, comm arrangement configuration..

My problem is that, once all this is done, when I try to execute my API from POSTMAN, I am not able to get the header AND the item data AT THE SAME TIME.

I am able for instance to get data for the header custom entity CDS view with the correct structure I defined: 

I am also able to get data for the line_item custom entity CDS view, for the different batch numbers to use for the consumption as per the structure I designed:

But, How can I merge these two results of these 2 entities in the same response?

I want my custom API response to be similar to the following one: 

How can I accomplish this please?

I thought by creating a composition relationship between these 2 custom entities, that the SELECT method will fetch data for both header and Item custom entity CDS views and that the result will be merged in the same response, but it is not.

Also, when I run a GET OData call to the header entity, "SELECT" method of class "ZCL_TEST_BATCH_NBR_DET0" is called only once, and "io_request->get_entity_id( )" gives "ZC_TEST_BATCH_DETERMIN_HD" as entity. This means, that my logic is being executed ONLY on the header custom CDS view, which is not enough to fulfill my requirement.

Then I tried to add "$expand" to the OData URL to execute, by adding "?$expand=_BatchDetItemDetails" to my URL:

but I am getting HTTP Bad Request error in POSTMAN : "

"The expand option's expand path contains an invalid property '_BatchDetItemDetails'",

Can someone help me on this please? I feel like my custom API is not designed to "accept" any "expand" attribute. 

Or if you have another idea on how to achieve this, without having to use the "?$expand" statement?

PS: the metadata of this custom API is attached to the present message

Thanks experts

entry.title: Load balancer for multiple SAP integration suite tenant
entry.content: Hi Experts, We are currently facing performance bottlenecks with our SAP Integration Suite tenant. We are exploring options to optimize performance or implement disaster recovery by provisioning additional instances. We came across a three-year-old SAP Community post Maximum Number of Messages for SAP Cloud Platform Integration indicating that Autoscaler Service support for SAP Integration Suite was on the roadmap. but not clear if this functionality has been implemented, If so, is there any guidance or documentation on how to configure the Autoscaler Service with SAP Integration Suite to enable automatic scaling based on workload or to support multi-instance disaster recovery. If this feature is not yet available, could you recommend alternative methods to address performance bottlenecks or achieve disaster recovery for SAP Integration Suite on BTP? For exam, is there any solution about the load balancer implemented for multiple CPI tenant regarding the external requests control? Any answer will be warm welcome. thanks in advance. Regards, Grady

entry.title: App "API Business Hub Experience" is not accessible from "SAP Integration Suite" for my trial accont
entry.content:
Hi

According to chapter in course "Developing with SAP Integration Suite" https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/developing-with-sap-integration-suite/creating-a-product_fc942de6-ff7b-4263-bd2b-f33fe481bc3d
One shall be able to see and access app/ecosystem "API Business Hub Experience".

But that is not visible for me.

The AuthGroup-rolecollections are in place for my user as I see it:

  • AuthGroup.ContentAuthor
  • AuthGroup.Site.Admin
  • AuthGroup.Content.Admin
  • AuthGroup.APIPortalRegistration
  • AuthGroup.API.ApplicationDeveloper
  • AuthGroup.API.Admin

Anyone else have same issue? Is something missing in my BTP setup perhaps?

 

Expected to see this when hovering over "Ecosystem" in SAP Integration Suite:

This is what I see instead:

 

Overview of instances in my SAP BTP Cockpit:

 

/Regards

Joakim

entry.title: Do we have any standard APIs available in SAP PI (NWDS) to retrieve message logs
entry.content:
Hi All, I'm working on automating some monitoring/reporting tasks and was wondering if SAP PI (NWDS) provides any standard APIs to programmatically access message logs and retrieve a list of communication channels.

 

By using this below API I'm unable to retriew the Messages logs

https://<host>:<port>/mdt/messageoverviewqueryservlet?component=<component_name>&view=<view_type>&begin=<begin_datetime>&end=<end_datetime>&detailedStatus=true

entry.title: Need a step by step help create an API with APIM ,set Basic Authentication and OAuth v2 Policies
entry.content:
Hi,

I have iflow with endpoint (https://53535estr.trial.it-cpitrial06-rt.cfapps.us10-001.hana.ondemand.com/http/customerDataJMS),

I want to create an API using integration suite API management to secure with policies
1) Basic Authentication and
2) OAuth v2.

I want to create a custom user name :User1 and password: test123 and give to vendor to access the API and Can we do the same custom  user name and password for OAuth v2.
please help.

entry.title: Question about the parameters to use in the API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV/CreateUrlAsAttachment
entry.content:
Hello,

We are trying to generate an attached URL in the material document header object, using the standard API API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV with the CreateUrlAsAttachment entity. But, we are not able to get the API call to successfully generate the URL.

 The main limitation we found is identifying the correct parameters to include . Currently, we are making the  API call, but it returns a 400 error. Please,  find below the parameters we are using:

SemanticObject:''

BusinessObjectTypeName: 'BUS2017'

LinkedSAPObjectKey: '49000005022025'

Url:'https://www.google.com

UrlDescription: 'For

More Info'
MIMEType: 'text/url'
SAPObjectType: 'materialdocument'
HarmonizedDocumentType: 'SL1'

 

 
 
Something may be wrong with the businessobjectTypeName, and we are unsure whether the value we are using is correct. Additionally, we are uncertain about the values for LinkedSAPObjectKey and el SAPObjectType , as we have not been able to determine from the API documentation what should be passed in these parameters.
Thank you in advance

entry.title: Instance not showing up when doing a SAP mission
entry.content:
Hi

I am currently trying this SAP mission:

https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/protected/index.html#/missiondetail/3258/3327/?tab=projectboard

I am now in the part:

Design and test your first integration scenario

Part 2:

Expose Integration Flow Endpoint as API and Test the Flow

Under Part 3 Copy credentials from service key you should see a new instance here:

  1. In your SAP BTP subaccount, navigate to your service instance page. Choose Services > Instances and Subscriptions

All I can see is the Integration Suite under Subscriptions. The Instance part is blank.

Do you have an isea what is missing here or what I am doing wrong?

Thanks!

entry.title: Adding API Management to CPI
entry.content:
Our current instance of SAP CPI - is the sfsf edition and does not include API management. I was able to add Entity and Service assignments for API management using the consumption model with CPEA credits. 

However when i try to subscribe to the service, it fails to create the instance the errors are as below..

Service broker error: Service broker apimanagement-apiportal failed with: Service instance provision failed with error : The service instance creation failed as the API Management, API portal is not set up yet for tenant

Service broker error: Service broker apimanagement-devportal failed with: Provision failed with error : DevPortal Subscription is empty for subaccount ************* with : {}

I do not see any boosters for API management at the global level either. 

Can anyone help with this, i suspect its a licensing issue with the base Instance of CPI being SFSF edition, but would like a confirmation. 

Regards

Ravi Raman

entry.title: Implemented OAuth2 Policy in APIM Integration suite successfully, but Unable to Access the iflow.
entry.content:
I created a new api proxy for Oauth token (https://2we321f7trial-trial.integrationsuitetrial-apim.us10.hana.ondemand.com:443/2we321f7trial/newoauth/newtoken)  added a oAuth2 policy Named "GenerateOAuthToken" to Generate the token and other api proxy for an iflow (https://2we321f7trial-trial.integrationsuitetrial-apim.us10.hana.ondemand.com:443/2we321f7trial/ikramSendData) used oAuth2 policy named "VerifyOAuthAccessToken" to verify the token, I am able to generate the AccessToken from Postman using the oAuth2 Authorization, after that   payload will be sent to the IFLOW, but after Generating the  access Token and using it ,when I am sending the data it is  giving the error "401 Unauthorized"

Code for oAuth2 policy "GenerateOAuthToken" 

<OAuthV2 async="false" continueOnError="false" enabled="true" XMLNS="http://www.sap.com/apimgmt"><Operation>GenerateAccessToken</Operation>

<GenerateResponse enabled="true"/>

    <SupportedGrantTypes>

           <GrantType>client_credentials</GrantType>

       </SupportedGrantTypes>

   <Tokens/>

</OAuthV2>

Code for oAuth2 policy "GenerateOAuthToken" 

<OAuthV2 async="false" continueOnError="false" enabled="true" XMLNS="http://www.sap.com/apimgmt">

  <Operation>VerifyAccessToken</Operation>

   <GenerateResponse enabled="true"/><SupportedGrantTypes/>

<Tokens/>

</OAuthV2>

Please suggest remedy to solve 401 UnAuthorized, so that the integration IFLOW  is accessed and data is sent.
